    }), "\n", _jsx(_components.p, {
      children: "The REST endpoints can wrap any Postgres query you like, including row-level security, computed columns, and stored procedures. The same pattern applies to MongoDB, Supabase, or a service mesh in front of multiple databases."
    }), "\n", _jsx(_components.h2, {
      id: "when-powersync-makes-sense",

      children: "PowerSync is a strong fit when:"

      children: ["\n", _jsx(_components.li, {
        children: "The team is SQL-first and wants to keep writing SQL on both client and server."

        children: "The backend is already Postgres or Mongo and you want managed CDC without writing pull and push handlers."
      }), "\n", _jsx(_components.li, {
        children: "The product targets Flutter, Kotlin, or Swift in addition to web, and you want a single vendor SDK across all of them."
      }), "\n", _jsx(_components.li, {
        children: "The license terms are acceptable for the product you are building."
      }), "\n"]
    }), "\n", _jsx(_components.p, {
      children: "If those constraints match, PowerSync gives you a coherent path. If you need browser-first performance, a NoSQL query model, custom backends, or unrestricted licensing, RxDB is the better tool."

      id: "faq",

        children: "Why is RxDB faster in the browser?"
      }), _jsxs(_components.p, {
        children: ["RxDB queries run in JavaScript directly against ", _jsx(_components.a, {
          href: "/rx-storage-indexeddb.html",
          children: "IndexedDB"
        }), " or ", _jsx(_components.a, {
          href: "/rx-storage-opfs.html",
          children: "OPFS"
        }), ", so each read returns typed objects without crossing a WASM boundary or decoding a SQLite result set. PowerSync executes queries inside a WASM SQLite build that persists to the same browser primitives, which adds extra serialization on every read and write. See ", _jsx(_components.a, {
          href: "/slow-indexeddb.html",

      })]
    }), "\n", _jsxs(Details, {
      children: [_jsx("summary", {

        children: ["Yes. RxDB does not ship a built-in Postgres connector, but the ", _jsx(_components.a, {
          href: "/replication-http.html",
          children: "HTTP replication"

      }), _jsx(_components.p, {
        children: "PowerSync is source-available under the Functional Source License (FSL), which permits non-competing use and converts to Apache 2.0 two years after each release. There is also a hosted cloud tier with usage-based pricing. RxDB core is Apache 2.0 with no field-of-use restriction."
